name: Nogales Monastery Ruins Loop
name_original: Alcubilla de Nogales desde San Esteban de Nogales — circular por el río Ería
description: Hike a moderate 7.6-mile circular trail from San Esteban de Nogales, following the Río Ería and passing historical ruins.
region_intro: Hiking around San Esteban De Nogales offers exploration of a landscape shaped by the Eria River and extensive forests. The village is situated at an elevation of 782 meters, providing varied terrain within the comarca of La Valdería in León, Spain. The region features woodlands dominated by False Acacias and Poplars, alongside historical sites like the Royal Monastery of Santa María de Nogales. This blend of riparian environments, forests, and historical landmarks defines the hiking experience. Best hiking trails around San Esteban De Nogales The most popular hiking route is Villanueva de Jamuz from Santa Elena de Jamuz — circular by the Río Jamúz, a 7.0 miles (11.2 km) trail that takes 2 hours 52 minutes to complete. This moderate route follows the Río Jamúz, offering riverside views. Another top favourite among local hikers is Nogales Monastery Ruins Loop, a moderate 7.6 miles (12.3 km) path. This trail explores the area around the historic Royal Monastery of Santa María de Nogales. Local hikers also love the Arrabalde — circular through the Sierra de las Labradas, a 5.3 miles (8.5 km) trail leading through the Sierra de las Labradas, often completed in about 2 hours 29 minutes. Hiking around San Esteban De Nogales is defined by river valleys, extensive forests, and historical ruins. The network offers options for different ability levels, including 3 easy and 5 moderate routes. The guide includes highly-rated routes that showcase the best of the region's landscape. More than 4 hikers have used komoot to explore San Esteban De Nogales's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available around San Esteban De Nogales?
A: There are 8 hiking routes around San Esteban De Nogales, offering a mix of difficulties. You'll find 3 easy trails and 5 moderate options to explore the region's diverse landscape.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on hikes near San Esteban De Nogales?
A: The hiking terrain around San Esteban De Nogales is varied, characterized by the presence of the Eria River and extensive woodlands. You'll encounter paths through forests dominated by False Acacias and Poplars, as well as routes along riparian environments. The village itself sits at 782 meters above sea level, contributing to a diverse landscape.

Q: Are there any easy or beginner-friendly hikes in the area?
A: Yes, San Esteban De Nogales offers 3 easy hiking routes suitable for beginners or those looking for a relaxed walk. An example is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1271723059/castrocalbon-circular-route-via-the-castle-and" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castrocalbón Circular Route via the Castle and&lt;/a&gt;, which is just under 4 km long.

Q: Are there any circular hiking routes around San Esteban De Nogales?
A: Yes, several circular routes are available. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1305549898/arrabalde-circular-through-the-sierra-de-las-labradas" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Arrabalde — circular through the Sierra de las Labradas&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 8.5 km loop. Another option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1305910124/castro-de-las-labradas-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castro de las Labradas Loop&lt;/a&gt;, a longer moderate trail of about 14 km.

Q: What historical landmarks or natural features can I see along the hiking trails?
A: The region is rich in both history and nature. Many trails offer views of the Eria River and lead through forests. A notable historical site is the Royal Monastery of Santa María de Nogales, which you can explore on the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1272045033/nogales-monastery-ruins-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Nogales Monastery Ruins Loop&lt;/a&gt;. Other nearby attractions include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6352862" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ruins of the Royal Monastery of Santa María de Nogales&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5786385" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castillo de los Pimentel&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any specific trails that follow the Eria River?
A: The Eria River is a significant natural feature in the area, shaping the landscape. While specific trails are not exclusively named after the Eria, the presence of the river means many routes, particularly those in riparian environments, will offer scenic views and paths alongside it. The traditional yearly construction of an alder wood bridge across the river for the San Jorge festivities highlights its integral role in the community.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in San Esteban De Nogales?
A: San Esteban De Nogales offers pleasant hiking conditions for much of the year. Spring and autumn generally provide mild temperatures and beautiful scenery, with the forests of False Acacias and Poplars displaying vibrant colors. Summer can be warm, making early morning or late afternoon hikes ideal. Winter hikes are also possible, though conditions may vary.

Q: Are the hiking trails in San Esteban De Nogales suitable for families with children?
A: Yes, with 3 easy routes available, there are options suitable for families. These shorter, less challenging trails allow families to enjoy the natural beauty of the Eria River and the surrounding woodlands without extensive effort. Always check the specific route details for elevation and distance to ensure it matches your family's capabilities.

Q: Are dogs allowed on the hiking trails around San Esteban De Nogales?
A: Generally, dogs are welcome on hiking trails in this region, especially on public footpaths and in natural areas. However, it's always advisable to keep your dog on a leash, particularly in areas with livestock or wildlife, and to follow local regulations regarding pet waste. There are no specific restrictions mentioned for the trails in this guide.

Q: Are there any cafes or places to eat near the hiking trails in San Esteban De Nogales?
A: San Esteban De Nogales is a small village, and while specific cafes directly on the trails are unlikely, you can find local establishments within the village itself or in nearby towns. It's recommended to check local listings or plan to bring your own refreshments, especially for longer hikes, as amenities might be limited directly along the routes.

Q: What do other hikers enjoy most about hiking in San Esteban De Nogales?
A: Hikers appreciate the blend of natural beauty and historical significance in San Esteban De Nogales. The komoot community has explored the varied terrain, often highlighting the peaceful riverside paths along the Eria River and the opportunity to visit historical sites like the Royal Monastery of Santa María de Nogales. The extensive forests also provide a refreshing hiking experience.
tips: The small town of San Esteban De Nogales sits on the banks of the Eria River. The town church is a good place to start your adventures in this area.
Nature is reclaiming its place around the ruins of this 16th and 17th century monastery. The building was looted several times and eventually fell into disrepair.

A nature walk around the Eria river. The river runs through the provinces of León and Zamora. It begins in the Teleno mountains of the León region. The Salix trees lining the river banks whistling in the wind.

faqs.question: How long does it take to hike this circular trail?
faqs.answer: The hike is estimated to take approximately 3 hours and 14 minutes to complete. This duration is for the full 12.3 km circular route.

faqs.question: What is the difficulty level of this hike?
faqs.answer: This trail is classified as moderate. It's generally considered to be of medium or average difficulty, suitable for hikers with some experience.

faqs.question: What kind of terrain can I expect on the route?
faqs.answer: The trail follows the Río Ería through diverse landscapes. You can expect to encounter meadows, some rocky areas, and groves of pine and poplar trees along its course in the region of Alcubilla de Nogales.

faqs.question: What interesting sights or landmarks can I see along the way?
faqs.answer: Along the route, you'll pass by the Church of San Esteban de Nogales and the Ruins of the Royal Monastery of Santa María de Nogales. The Eria River in Alcubilla de Nogales itself is a significant feature, known for its transparent waters and historical connection to gold panning.

faqs.question: Is this trail dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: Yes, this trail is generally considered dog-friendly. However, it's always recommended to keep your dog on a leash, especially when passing through villages or near livestock, and to follow local regulations.

faqs.question: Do I need a permit or have to pay an entrance fee for this hike?
faqs.answer: No, there are no specific permits or entrance fees required to hike this trail. It is freely accessible to the public.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access the trailhead?
faqs.answer: While specific parking areas aren't detailed, you would typically look for parking in or around San Esteban de Nogales, as this is the starting point for the circular route. Street parking is often available in smaller villages.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to hike this route?
faqs.answer: The trail can be enjoyed throughout much of the year. Spring and autumn offer pleasant temperatures and vibrant scenery. Summer can be warm, so an early start is advisable, while winter might present colder conditions, though the transparent waters of the Río Ería are always a sight.

faqs.question: Does this trail connect with any other well-known paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, sections of this route intersect with other paths. You will find it passes through parts of the Camino A-1, Camino A-3, Camino de Alcubilla a Alija (Camino A), Camino A-1-2, Camino A-2, Camino E-2, and Camino A-5.

faqs.question: What makes the Río Ería particularly interesting?
faqs.answer: The Río Ería is notable for its transparent waters and its historical significance. Originating in the Teleno mountains, it was historically one of the richest gold-bearing rivers in the León province. Even today, gold panning workshops are held where participants can still find gold nuggets.

faqs.question: Are there any amenities like cafes or accommodation near the trailhead in San Esteban de Nogales?
faqs.answer: San Esteban de Nogales is a small village. While specific establishments aren't listed, you may find local cafes or guesthouses in the village or nearby larger towns in the Benavente y Los Valles region of Zamora, Castile and León, Spain.
